title: Wide Bookcase Base — Modular Office Collection
summary: A shallow, wide plywood bookcase with double top and bottom trim — sized to serve as a desk base or stand on its own.
description:
This bookcase is deliberately shallow and wide rather than tall — 15-1/4" deep — which is what makes it work as a desk base as easily as a bookcase; a shallower box gives you legroom underneath if you set a desktop across two of these. The double-thickness trim at the top and bottom (a 1x2 layered with a 1x3) is what gives the edges enough visual weight that the case doesn't read as a plain plywood box, and it's applied inside the front opening as well as around the outside.

Rip your whole sheet of plywood to 15-1/4" wide before cutting anything to length — every solid panel in this case shares that depth, so getting the rip consistent up front saves you from chasing small width mismatches between parts later.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF or A1 plywood, ripped 15-1/4" wide, 8 ft sheet

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Fasten the shelves to the sides, then fasten the top to the sides, using 2" self-tapping screws (or 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws).
materials: 3/4" MDF or A1 plywood, ripped 15-1/4" wide, 8 ft sheet

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Fasten the back to the bookcase with 1-1/4" nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the inside front trim
steps.body: Fasten the top and bottom inside front trim pieces. Use 2" finish nails, nailing through the sides and top/bottom shelves into the edges of the trim pieces.

steps.title: Add the side and outer front trim, then finish
steps.body: Fasten the top and bottom side trim pieces, then the top and bottom front trim pieces the same way, keeping the layered 1x2-over-1x3 look consistent all the way around. Fill nail holes with wood filler, sand, and finish.

images.alt: Wide, shallow plywood bookcase base for a modular office desk
